codeReview.summary: Implementation fully covers the feature requirements. The blok table has all 4 new columns (pokok INTEGER, sph REAL, bulan_tanam TEXT, status_tanaman TEXT). STATUS_OPTIONS correctly changed to ['TM', 'TBM', 'TTM', 'TLS']. All affected functions (getBlokCount, createSampleData, createBlok, updateBlok, importBlokBatch) handle the new columns correctly. Typecheck passes, lint shows only pre-existing warnings.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The Blok interface uses camelCase for the returned object's bulan_tanam and status_tanaman properties (bulan_tanam: bulanTanam, status_tanaman: statusTanaman) but the database schema uses snake_case (bulan_tanam, status_tanaman). This inconsistency is handled correctly in the implementation but could be confusing. Not a bug since the mapping is intentional.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/main/blokDatabase.ts:248-251 maps function parameter bulanTanam to database column bulan_tanam

sharedStateObservations.observation: The feature description mentions getBlokCount should be updated, but getBlokCount is a private helper function that only returns a count - it doesn't need modification to handle new columns. The implementation correctly leaves it unchanged.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: getBlokCount() uses 'SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM blok' which naturally works with any schema

summary: blok-database-schema-update feature passes review. Git diff shows clean implementation with 88 insertions and 31 deletions. The Blok interface updated with 4 new nullable fields (pokok, sph, bulan_tanam, status_tanaman). STATUS_OPTIONS changed from ['aktif', 'tidak_aktif', 'dalam_perawatan'] to ['TM', 'TBM', 'TTM', 'TLS']. All CRUD functions (createBlok, updateBlok) and importBlokBatch properly handle the new columns with validation. TypeScript compilation passes. Only pre-existing lint warnings present (not related to this feature).
